title: Finding the Whole When Given the Part and Percent

steps.instruction: 24 is 60% of what number? Identify the part and the percent.
steps.explanation: In percent problems there are three quantities: part, percent, and whole. We need to find the whole.

steps.instruction: Use the formula: part = percent × whole.
steps.explanation: We substitute the known values into the percent equation.

title: Finding the Percent When Given the Part and Whole

steps.instruction: A class has 32 students. 8 of them are left-handed. What percent are left-handed?
steps.explanation: We know the part (8 left-handed) and the whole (32 students).
Dividing the part by the whole gives the percent as a decimal.

steps.work: 0.25 × 100 = 25%. So 25% of the class is left-handed.
steps.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percent, multiply by 100 (or move the decimal point two places right).
